Vata.summary: Vata governs movement, creativity, and change in the body. When balanced, it brings vitality and flexibility; when aggravated, it leads to anxiety, dryness, and irregular digestion.

favor: Healthy fats like ghee and sesame oil
reduce: Bitter, pungent, and astringent tastes in excess

daily_routine.notes: Vata benefits most from consistency — regular meal times and a steady daily rhythm help reduce anxiety and restlessness.

exercise.notes: Avoid overexertion or highly intense workouts, which can aggravate Vata further.

Vata.seasonal_note: Vata tends to aggravate in autumn and early winter; extra warmth, oil massage (abhyanga), and routine are especially helpful during this season.

Pitta.summary: Pitta governs digestion, metabolism, and transformation. When balanced, it brings sharp intellect and strong digestion; when aggravated, it leads to irritability, inflammation, and acidity.

favor: Fresh fruits like melon, coconut, and grapes

daily_routine.notes: Pitta benefits from avoiding the hottest part of the day for intense activity or heavy meals; midday is best for the main meal since digestion is strongest then.

exercise.notes: Exercise during cooler parts of the day; avoid competitive or overly intense workouts that raise internal heat and irritability.

Pitta.seasonal_note: Pitta tends to aggravate in summer; cooling foods, shade, and avoiding midday sun are especially helpful during this season.

Kapha.summary: Kapha governs structure, stability, and lubrication in the body. When balanced, it brings strength and calm; when aggravated, it leads to sluggishness, weight gain, and congestion.

favor: Spices like ginger, black pepper, and turmeric
Honey in moderation instead of sugar
reduce: Sweet and salty tastes in excess
Overeating or eating late at night

daily_routine.notes: Kapha benefits from an energizing start to the day; avoid oversleeping, which increases heaviness and sluggishness.

exercise.notes: Morning exercise works especially well for Kapha, helping to counter natural sluggishness and stimulate metabolism for the day.

Kapha.seasonal_note: Kapha tends to aggravate in late winter and spring; lighter foods and more vigorous activity are especially helpful during this season.
